system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: A regional highway expansion threatens to cut off District A's only commercial block from its residential core. Budget and zone constraints apply.
phase_objective: Restore commercial access to residential blocks without losing any housing capacity or open space.

expert_response.rationale: The highway severs B3 from B1/B2. To restore commercial access, I can create a new commercial node at the unzoned B5, which is adjacent to both residential blocks. I zone B5 as commercial and develop it with retail. B3 can remain as commercial for future use or be redeveloped later. No housing or green space is lost.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Creates a new commercial node without touching any existing residential or open space, maintaining all targets. Demonstrates long‑horizon replanning.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Redeveloping the park (B4) into commercial would violate greenery; moving housing away would break the housing constraint.

Budget and zone constraints apply."}

B3 can remain as commercial for future use or be redeveloped later. No housing or green space is lost."}

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: A heritage building is discovered on B4, capping its density. The district still needs total housing capacity of 5.
phase_objective: Restore housing_capacity >= 5 while respecting the heritage classification on B4.

expert_response.rationale: B4 is now is_protected=true. The verifier's redevelop forces protected blocks to become open_space regardless of the requested `use`, so the cleanest legal move is redevelop(B4, use=null) — this clears the housing, frees infra, and turns B4 into a heritage-protected green parcel that also boosts greenery_ratio. To recover the lost housing slot I zone B6 residential and develop it with use=housing. B2, B3, B5 remain untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Selectively fixes the heritage block under the real verifier rule (protected blocks can only become open_space), compensates by upzoning a nearby vacant block, and preserves untouched B2/B3/B5. Housing capacity is restored and greenery_ratio rises.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Trying redevelop(B4, use='institutional') would still result in B4 becoming open_space (the protected branch fires before the use is honoured), wasting a turn. Bulldozing the park or upzoning the commercial block would disrupt other metrics for no gain.

The district still needs total housing capacity of 5."}
sft_output: {"actions":[{"action_type":"redevelop","block_id":"B4","use":null},{"action_type":"set_zoning","block_id":"B6","zone":"residential"},{"action_type":"develop","block_id":"B6","use":"housing"}],"rationale":"B4 is now is_protected=true. The verifier's redevelop forces protected blocks to become open_space regardless of the requested `use`, so the cleanest legal move is redevelop(B4, use=null) — this clears the housing, frees infra, and turns B4 into a heritage-protected green parcel that also boosts greenery_ratio. To recover the lost housing slot I zone B6 residential and develop it with use=housing. B2, B3, B5 remain untouched."}

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: A demographic shift makes the fire station on B9 underutilised and creates a sharp rise in clinic demand. Adapt the district while keeping coverage high.
phase_objective: service_coverage >= 0.8 with appropriate amenities for current population.

expert_response.rationale: I redevelop B9, replacing the obsolete fire station use with institutional (keeping the civic zone) and then assign a clinic to the same block. This reuse of existing infrastructure restores service coverage to 100% without disturbing residential blocks.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Minimal‑footprint replanning: adapts an existing civic block to a new need, leaving housing untouched and meeting the coverage target.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Demolishing the whole block or building a new clinic on a distant block would waste resources and fail to cover the residential population.

Adapt the district while keeping coverage high."}

This reuse of existing infrastructure restores service coverage to 100% without disturbing residential blocks."}

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: A demographic shift makes the fire station on B9 underutilised and creates a clinic deficiency.
phase_objective: Replace the obsolete fire amenity on B9 with a clinic to lift district_service_gap_count to zero.

expert_response.rationale: B9 is civic+institutional with the fire amenity. assign_amenity on a block that already has an amenity is REJECTED, so the only legal swap is to clear the existing amenity first. redevelop(B9, use='institutional') reuses the same use but, in clearing and re-applying the use, it also nulls out the amenity (per the verifier's redevelop logic), freeing the slot. I then assign_amenity clinic on the same block — civic zone allows clinic, the block is developed as institutional, and verifier co-locates without re-allocating infra. B1 stays untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Minimal-footprint replanning: reuses the existing civic anchor, satisfies the new demand, and lifts district_service_gap_count by one. affected_set_recall is 1.0 with zero unnecessary touches outside B9.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: assign_amenity clinic on B9 directly would be REJECTED ('block already has amenity fire'). Demolishing B9 entirely (e.g. redevelop with use=null on a non-protected/non-flood block) would be REJECTED for the same reason — redevelop with use=null is only allowed on flood/heritage blocks. Building a fresh civic block elsewhere would waste land and infra.

sft_output: {"actions":[{"action_type":"redevelop","block_id":"B9","use":"institutional"},{"action_type":"assign_amenity","amenity_type":"clinic","block_id":"B9"}],"rationale":"B9 is civic+institutional with the fire amenity. assign_amenity on a block that already has an amenity is REJECTED, so the only legal swap is to clear the existing amenity first. redevelop(B9, use='institutional') reuses the same use but, in clearing and re-applying the use, it also nulls out the amenity (per the verifier's redevelop logic), freeing the slot. I then assign_amenity clinic on the same block — civic zone allows clinic, the block is developed as institutional, and verifier co-locates without re-allocating infra. B1 stays untouched."}

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: A new policy demands at least 30% greenery and more open space. The district currently has a park on B2, but needs more green while keeping housing capacity.
phase_objective: greenery_ratio >= 0.30, housing_capacity >= 3.

expert_response.rationale: Current greenery is 1 out of 5 blocks (20%). I need at least 30%, so I add one more open space. I convert B4 into open_space, raising greenery to 40%. To meet the housing target, I zone and develop B5 as residential. The existing blocks remain untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Balances two competing constraints surgically: adds green to an empty block, adds housing on another empty block, preserving all existing uses.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Rezoning existing residential to open space would lose housing unnecessarily; ignoring greenery fails the new requirement.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: The TOD district has reached all Phase 3 targets early. No further actions are needed; the planner should end the phase.
phase_objective: greenery_ratio >= 0.10, housing_capacity >= 4, service_coverage >= 1.0, transit_access of hub = 100.

expert_response.rationale: All metrics exceed requirements: greenery 11% > 10%, housing 4, service_coverage 1.0, hub transit upgraded. No more actions are beneficial. I defer to conclude the phase.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Shows ability to recognize completion and avoid over-building; defer is the correct minimal action.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Adding unnecessary amenities or rezoning would waste budget and risk breaking existing balance.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: Transform a 3x3 grid around a central transit hub into a high-density transit-oriented district.
phase_objective: Zone the hub and adjacent blocks to maximise future housing capacity near transit.

expert_response.rationale: TOD principle: highest activity at the transit core, high-density residential immediately adjacent. I zone B5 (centre) commercial to anchor the hub, and the four orthogonal neighbours (B2, B4, B6, B8) residential to feed walk-up density. Corner blocks (B1, B3, B7, B9) remain unzoned for later open space / mixed use, so the outer ring stays flexible for the next phase.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Follows TOD principles: high-activity commercial at the transit core, high-capacity residential immediately adjacent, leaving the outer ring for future open space or services.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Zoning low-density residential next to the hub or putting high-density blocks far from the centre would undermine the TOD goal; zoning all nine blocks now leaves no flexibility for the services_and_access phase.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: Transform a 3×3 grid around a central transit hub into a high‑density transit‑oriented district.
phase_objective: Zone the hub and adjacent blocks to maximise future housing capacity near transit.

expert_response.rationale: I place commercial at the hub (B5) and residential on all adjacent blocks with high transit_access (B2,B4,B6,B8). This maximises TOD density. Outer blocks remain unzoned for later green or mixed uses.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Follows TOD principles: high-activity commercial at the transit core, high-capacity residential immediately adjacent.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Placing low-density residential next to the station or high-density blocks far from transit would undermine the goal.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: After TOD zoning, enhance walkability and health coverage.
phase_objective: Place a clinic that covers the residential cluster and add a green block to satisfy greenery_ratio >= 0.15.

expert_response.rationale: Clinic cannot sit on a residential block (verifier requires civic/mixed/commercial). B5 is the central commercial hub already developed as retail, so co-locating a clinic there gives walking-radius coverage to the four residential neighbours without spending a fresh block. For greenery, I reserve the corner B3 — keeping the residential ring intact and pushing greenery_ratio from 0.0 to ~0.11; combined with later phases this will clear the 15% floor as the unzoned outer corners follow the same pattern.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Co-locates the clinic on a developed commercial block (verifier preserves use=retail and stamps amenity=clinic without re-allocating infra), and adds open_space on a corner to start meeting greenery without disturbing the TOD residential ring.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: assign_amenity clinic on B4 (residential) would be REJECTED. Reserving a residential block as open_space would destroy housing capacity. Placing the clinic on the far edge (B9) would leave the central residential cluster uncovered.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: After TOD zoning, develop the residential blocks and add a clinic to improve health coverage.
phase_objective: All residential blocks must be developed with housing, and service_coverage for clinics must be at least 0.8.

expert_response.rationale: I develop all four residential blocks with housing. To provide clinic access, I place a clinic on the central commercial block B5, which is adjacent to every residential block, achieving full coverage. I also reserve B3 as open_space to begin meeting greenery needs.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Completes all residential development, strategically places a crucial amenity to maximise coverage, and initiates greenery.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Placing clinics only on individual residential blocks would waste amenity slots and not achieve the coverage requirement efficiently.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: The TOD district needs a grocery store accessible to all residential blocks and a transit upgrade on the hub.
phase_objective: service_coverage >= 1.0 for grocery; transit_access of hub and adjacent blocks must be 100.

expert_response.rationale: I place a grocery on the central commercial block B5, which is adjacent to all residential blocks, achieving full coverage. Then I upgrade the transit infrastructure in the hub zone (block B5) to full capacity, raising transit_access for adjacent blocks.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Leverages the central block for multiple functions, meeting both amenity and infrastructure targets without disturbing residential or open_space.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Putting a grocery on a single residential block leaves others uncovered; failing to upgrade transit leaves the district less accessible.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: District 2 was planned for housing and greenery. A flood moratorium suddenly applies to high-risk blocks.
phase_objective: Clear any developed block that became flood-risk; preserve greenery >= 0.20; touch only the affected block.

expert_response.rationale: B1 became flood-risk after development — that is now an active constraint violation. The verifier's redevelop action accepts use=null on a flood-risk or protected block: it frees the infra allocation, sets zone=open_space and reserved_open_space=True in one shot. B2 is unaffected and must stay untouched to preserve the affected_set_precision metric.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Selectively fixes the single invalid block with the minimum-cost legal verb. The flood-risk shortcut means one action both clears the use and converts the block to a flood-compatible green buffer — greenery rises to 1.0 and the affected_set_recall is 1.0 with zero unnecessary touches.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Ignoring the moratorium leaves a constraint violation each step; rezoning the whole district drops affected_set_precision to near-zero and racks up unnecessary_change_count; calling reserve_open_space on a developed block is REJECTED — only undeveloped or use=='park' blocks accept it.

sft_input: {"active_constraints":["flood_moratorium_enacted","min_greenery_20","selective_replanning"],"current_phase":"replanning","legal_actions_summary":["redevelop(block_id, use)","reserve_open_space(block_id)","defer(block_id, phase_id)"],"observation_summary":{"blocks":[{"amenity":null,"block_id":"B1","district":"I1","has_road_access":true,"infra_zone":"I1","is_floodrisk":true,"is_protected":false,"phase":0,"use":"housing","zone":"residential"},{"amenity":"park","block_id":"B2","district":"I1","has_road_access":true,"infra_zone":"I1","is_floodrisk":false,"is_protected":false,"phase":0,"use":"park","zone":"open_space"}],"district_stats":{"amenity_coverage":{"park":1},"blocks_by_use":{"housing":1,"park":1},"greenery_ratio":0.5}},"phase_objective":"Clear any developed block that became flood-risk; preserve greenery >= 0.20; touch only the affected block.","task_briefing":"District 2 was planned for housing and greenery. A flood moratorium suddenly applies to high-risk blocks."}
sft_output: {"actions":[{"action_type":"redevelop","block_id":"B1","use":null}],"rationale":"B1 became flood-risk after development — that is now an active constraint violation. The verifier's redevelop action accepts use=null on a flood-risk or protected block: it frees the infra allocation, sets zone=open_space and reserved_open_space=True in one shot. B2 is unaffected and must stay untouched to preserve the affected_set_precision metric."}

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: District 2 was planned for housing and greenery. A flood moratorium suddenly prohibits any use other than park on high‑risk blocks.
phase_objective: Ensure all uses are legal under the new moratorium and greenery remains ≥20%.

expert_response.rationale: B1's housing use is now illegal; the only allowed use for a high‑risk block is park. I redevelop B1 to park use (open_space zone is inferred by the env). B2 remains untouched as it is already compliant.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Selectively fixes the single illegal usage while preserving the valid block. Greenery ratio remains high.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Ignoring the moratorium and keeping B1 as housing would be illegal. Rezoning the entire district wastes resources.

task_briefing: A sudden policy requires every residential block to have a clinic nearby. A previously unserved block must be retrofitted.
phase_objective: service_coverage must reach 1.0 (all residential blocks have a clinic within 1 block).

expert_response.rationale: B1 already has a grocery, B2 is unserved. I could place a clinic on B2 itself, but that blocks future housing use. Instead, I place a clinic on the mixed block B3, which is adjacent to both residential blocks, instantly reaching 100% coverage without altering housing.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Surgically adds an amenity to an existing non‑residential block to fix a coverage gap, leaving the housing blocks intact.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Placing the clinic on B2 would consume the only un‑serviced residential block's amenity slot and might violate future development plans.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: District 3 has a commercial core, but a target_override curveball requires reducing infra demand without losing economic activity.
phase_objective: Reduce I1 infra usage by at least one heavy-block worth while keeping at least one commercial use.

expert_response.rationale: Wait — the current verifier's redevelop only accepts a new `use` (it cannot change `zone` in one step), so I cannot turn commercial+retail into mixed+retail in a single action. The cleanest single-action fix that frees infra without dropping below 'one commercial use' is to swap B2's heavy retail for a lighter compatible use within the same commercial zone: office. That preserves commercial activity and shaves infra cost (office demand is lower than retail in this scenario). B1 and B3 stay untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Surgical fix: only the cost-heavy block is changed, the economic function is preserved (commercial+office still satisfies 'retain_commercial'), and verifier rules are respected. redevelop frees the old infra demand and re-allocates the new use's lower demand in one step.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Trying to redevelop B2 with new_zone='mixed' would be REJECTED — the action has no new_zone field. Demolishing B3 (the park) would violate the greenery floor; rezoning B1 from residential to commercial would lose housing capacity and disturb an unaffected block.

sft_input: {"active_constraints":["i1_capacity_tight","min_greenery_20","retain_commercial"],"current_phase":"replanning","legal_actions_summary":["redevelop(block_id, use)","set_zoning(block_id, zone)","defer(block_id, phase_id)"],"observation_summary":{"blocks":[{"amenity":"grocery","block_id":"B1","district":"I1","has_road_access":true,"infra_zone":"I1","is_floodrisk":false,"is_protected":false,"phase":0,"use":"housing","zone":"residential"},{"amenity":null,"block_id":"B2","district":"I1","has_road_access":true,"infra_zone":"I1","is_floodrisk":false,"is_protected":false,"phase":0,"use":"retail","zone":"commercial"},{"amenity":"park","block_id":"B3","district":"I1","has_road_access":true,"infra_zone":"I1","is_floodrisk":false,"is_protected":false,"phase":0,"use":"park","zone":"open_space"}],"district_stats":{"amenity_coverage":{"grocery":1,"park":1},"blocks_by_use":{"housing":1,"park":1,"retail":1},"greenery_ratio":0.33,"infra_zones":{"I1":{"power":[34,36],"sewer":[16,18],"water":[22,25]}}}},"phase_objective":"Reduce I1 infra usage by at least one heavy-block worth while keeping at least one commercial use.","task_briefing":"District 3 has a commercial core, but a target_override curveball requires reducing infra demand without losing economic activity."}
sft_output: {"actions":[{"action_type":"redevelop","block_id":"B2","use":"office"}],"rationale":"Wait — the current verifier's redevelop only accepts a new `use` (it cannot change `zone` in one step), so I cannot turn commercial+retail into mixed+retail in a single action. The cleanest single-action fix that frees infra without dropping below 'one commercial use' is to swap B2's heavy retail for a lighter compatible use within the same commercial zone: office. That preserves commercial activity and shaves infra cost (office demand is lower than retail in this scenario). B1 and B3 stay untouched."}

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: A heritage building is discovered on B2, a residential block. Housing is no longer allowed there, but the district still needs two housing units.
phase_objective: Keep housing_capacity >= 2 after resolving the heritage issue.

expert_response.rationale: B2's housing use must be removed; I redevelop it to institutional (civic zone stays). To recover the lost housing, I zone the vacant B3 as residential and develop it with housing. B1 stays unchanged.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Selectively replaces the illegal use and compensates with a new block to maintain the target, without touching the valid block.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Keeping housing on B2 violates heritage; converting the only remaining residential block to something else would drop capacity below target.

task_briefing: A fire destroyed the only school on B2. The neighborhood still needs a school, but must not lose housing capacity.
phase_objective: Restore school amenity coverage to 100% while keeping housing_capacity >= 3.

expert_response.rationale: The destroyed school was on B2, but B2 is now mixed/retail and housing capacity is already tight. Rather than redevelop a block, I place a new school on B1, which is residential and centrally located, covering all three residential blocks. No blocks lose housing.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Finds a non‑disruptive solution: adds the amenity to an existing residential block without destroying housing or rezoning.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Redeveloping B2 or B3 to re-add a school would lose either retail or housing capacity and worsen metrics.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Recognises an optimal state and prevents over‑tinkering, preserving a successful plan.

task_briefing: Develop District 1 into a small mixed‑use area with at least 20% greenery.
phase_objective: Zone at least 1 residential block and ensure greenery_ratio >= 0.20.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Directly fulfills the phase objective with two legal actions, using correct zone types and action payload fields.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Only zoning B1 residential leaves greenery at 0%; zoning B2 as industrial violates the greenery requirement.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: Develop District 1 into a small mixed-use area with at least 20% greenery and one housing block.
phase_objective: Establish at least one residential block and ensure greenery_ratio >= 0.20.

expert_response.rationale: Two simple moves cover both objectives: zone B1 residential to satisfy the housing requirement, and reserve B2 as open_space to push greenery_ratio to 0.50 — well above the 0.20 floor.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Directly fulfils the phase objective with two legal actions, using correct zone names and the dedicated reserve_open_space verb (which sets reserved_open_space=True so the block counts toward greenery_ratio).
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Only zoning B1 residential leaves greenery at 0%; zoning B2 industrial would violate the greenery requirement and (depending on adjacency) clash with the residential neighbour.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: Develop District 1 into a small mixed‑use area with at least 20% greenery.
phase_objective: Ensure every residential block has a grocery amenity within 1 block.

expert_response.rationale: Only B1 is residential and eligible for amenities. I assign a grocery store to B1, bringing service coverage to 100%.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Respects amenity placement rules, completes the phase objective without modifying the green space.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Assigning grocery to B2 (open_space) is illegal; rezoning the park to residential to add an amenity would destroy greenery.

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: Develop District 1 into a small mixed-use area with at least 20% greenery and one housing block.
phase_objective: Site a grocery amenity within walking distance of the residential cluster without disturbing the green space.

expert_response.rationale: B2 is the green buffer and must stay untouched. B1 is residential and cannot host a grocery amenity (verifier requires civic/mixed/commercial). I zone B3 commercial and assign a grocery amenity to it — assign_amenity on an undeveloped commercial block develops it as institutional with the grocery amenity stamp, giving walking-distance coverage to the housing on B1.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Respects amenity placement rules (commercial zone), preserves the open_space block, and adds the missing service to the district stats with the minimum possible footprint.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: assign_amenity grocery on B1 (residential) would be REJECTED by the verifier; reserving B3 as open_space would inflate greenery but leave the grocery target unmet; redeveloping B2 would destroy the greenery floor.

sft_output: {"actions":[{"action_type":"set_zoning","block_id":"B3","zone":"commercial"},{"action_type":"assign_amenity","amenity_type":"grocery","block_id":"B3"}],"rationale":"B2 is the green buffer and must stay untouched. B1 is residential and cannot host a grocery amenity (verifier requires civic/mixed/commercial). I zone B3 commercial and assign a grocery amenity to it — assign_amenity on an undeveloped commercial block develops it as institutional with the grocery amenity stamp, giving walking-distance coverage to the housing on B1."}

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: Complete Phase 1 for a two‑block district. The phase requires 1 residential block and the other block to be developed as a park.
phase_objective: Have one block zoned residential, the other reserved as open space, and at least one block developed with a use.

expert_response.rationale: I set B1 to residential and immediately develop it with housing. B2 is reserved as open space, automatically giving it park use and meeting the greenery target.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Shows proper sequencing: zoning before development, uses correct action payloads, and satisfies all phase goals.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Trying to develop without zoning is illegal; forgetting to develop means the phase isn't completed.

The phase requires 1 residential block and the other block to be developed as a park."}

B2 is reserved as open space, automatically giving it park use and meeting the greenery target."}

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: Site transit amenities along a 5-block commercial spine so the corridor reaches at least 60% transit coverage.
phase_objective: Achieve transit amenity coverage on at least 3 of 5 blocks; transit may be co-located on commercial blocks under the verifier's commercial-amenity rule.

expert_response.rationale: Transit is an amenity, not an infrastructure type — assign_amenity is the right verb. Spaced placement maximises walking radius: I co-locate transit on B1, B3, B5 (every other block). The verifier allows transit on commercial blocks and, since each is already developed (retail/office), it stamps the amenity without changing the use.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Uses the correct verb for siting a transit stop (assign_amenity, not upgrade_infrastructure), respects amenity-zone compatibility, and achieves the 60% target with the minimum number of co-located stamps.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: upgrade_infrastructure on a block id with type='transit' would be REJECTED — upgrade_infrastructure only accepts (infra_zone, infra_type, capacity) and infra_type must be water/sewer/power/road. Assigning transit to all five blocks would saturate amenity slots and waste the no_double_amenity budget.

Spaced placement maximises walking radius: I co-locate transit on B1, B3, B5 (every other block). The verifier allows transit on commercial blocks and, since each is already developed (retail/office), it stamps the amenity without changing the use."}

system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
task_briefing: District 2 already has two blocks zoned but undeveloped. Complete Phase 2 by developing them and adding a school.
phase_objective: All residential blocks must have a use (housing), and there must be at least one school amenity.

expert_response.rationale: I develop both residential blocks with housing. To meet the school requirement, I add a school on B1. Since B1 is adjacent to B2, both blocks are covered. B3 remains untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Sequences development before amenity, fulfills both constraints with minimal actions, and leaves open space intact.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Placing the school on B3 (open_space) is illegal. Forgetting to develop B2 would leave the phase incomplete.

Complete Phase 2 by developing them and adding a school."}
sft_output: {"actions":[{"action_type":"develop","block_id":"B1","use":"housing"},{"action_type":"develop","block_id":"B2","use":"housing"},{"action_type":"assign_amenity","amenity_type":"school","block_id":"B1"}],"rationale":"I develop both residential blocks with housing. To meet the school requirement, I add a school on B1. Since B1 is adjacent to B2, both blocks are covered. B3 remains untouched."}
